message.content: Adversaries may acquire SSL/TLS digital certificates through purchase or theft to enhance their operations. These certificates, which instill trust by verifying the owner's identity and key information, can be used to encrypt command and control (C2) traffic or facilitate Adversary-in-the-Middle attacks if trusted. Methods of acquisition include using front organizations, stolen information from compromised entities, or directly stealing from certificate authorities. Adversaries may also register or hijack domains to obtain certificates, sometimes for free from certain authorities. Notable groups like BlackTech, Lazarus Group, and Silent Librarian have exploited valid or stolen certificates for malware, C2 domains, and phishing pages. Mitigation strategies include monitoring and validating certificate issuance, employing strict certificate policies, and using certificate transparency logs to detect unauthorized certificates.
